calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Nov-29-2017 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-11-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jennifer Ramirez for Maria Greene (Executor).

The Court noted that there is a substantial request for extraordinary fees and stated that heir hunting is not typically an attorney function. Counsel stated that her Client lacked the expertise with respect to what was required of her, that there was a reverse mortgage, that the Client was unfamiliar with working with the mortgage company to prevent foreclosure and that there was a change in law regarding reverse mortgages. Ms. Ramirez also stated that they had to remove the notice of deficit and got the property sold, that the Attorney put in a lot of time in heir hunting, that they can look at there time again and that the Decedent left the property in poor condition. The Petition for Final Distribution, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-13-2017 Continued To Jun-27-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jun-01-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Yulissa Zulaica & Jennifer Ramirez for and with (petitioner) Maria Greene.

The Court stated we are here on a petition for probate, that there is a pending ex parte order to dispense with notice and that it needs a corrected proof of service. Ms. Ramirez stated that they will be filing the corrected notice this morning. The Court asked about the calls to Margarito and if they have obtained an address. Ms. Ramirez that they do, that Margarito has received a copy of the petition and that it was sent on 4/19/16 with a cover page in Spanish. The Court asked to see a copy of the notice and it was provided. Ms. Zulaica stated that Mr. Cuevas received a copy of the notice, petition and ex parte petition. Ms. Ramirez stated that there is $1,000,000.00 in the estate. Ms. Zulaica stated that there is $400,000.00 in cash, a reverse mortgage of $300,000.00 and property and that the total is 1.3 or 1.4 million and that there is a valid will. The Court asked if there are people that they know exist that they can find addresses for. Ms. Zulaica responded that there are 4 grand nephews but no addresses. The Court asked if they have tried calling relatives. Ms. Ramirez stated that the petition has tried to call Margarito and has sent correspondence with no response. The Court asked if these are the people that are the subject of the ex parte and are there any others that they know exist and are entitled to notice. Ms. Ramirez responded no, that the probate examiner raised the issue of the 1/2 siblings grandchildren but that no one knows of their existence. Ms. Zulaica stated that they can file an amended notice. The Court stated that it will take the petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary under submission to consider whether it is satisfied that we are in a position to go forward.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar May-11-2016 Continued To May-25-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
